metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace 'spot-check' with concrete proving commands for each AC: e.g., `curl -sI <badge-url> | grep '200 OK'` for AC2, `git diff HEAD~1 -- README.md | grep -c '^[+-]' | awk '$1 <= 4'` for AC4.
findings.finding: No acceptance criterion has a concrete proving command; AC2 uses the hedge 'spot-check' instead of a deterministic check, leaving URL-resolution verification unmeasurable and subjective.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2: 'All badge URLs resolve (spot-check rendered README on the mirror)'
findings.suggested_fix:
AC2: `for url in <badge-url-1> <badge-url-2> <badge-url-3> <badge-url-4>; do curl -sfI "$url" | head -1; done` — all must return HTTP 200.
AC4: `git diff HEAD -- README.md` must show only the added badge line (±1–3 lines near the top).
Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the GitHub mirror repo path (e.g., `github.com/navapbc/nava-rebar`) explicitly in the plan so the CI badge URL can be constructed without investigation.
findings.finding: The plan never states the GitHub mirror repository path (e.g., `github.com/<org>/<repo>`), which is required to construct the CI badge URL (`https://github.com/<org>/<repo>/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg`). An executing agent cannot form this URL from the plan text alone.
findings.location: What/files section — 'the test.yml workflow status badge from the GitHub mirror'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one line to the What/files section: 'GitHub mirror: `https://github.com/navapbc/nava-rebar` — CI badge URL: `https://github.com/navapbc/nava-rebar/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?branch=main`.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify that the GitHub Actions CI badge URL must include `?branch=main` (e.g. `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?branch=main`) so the badge is pinned to the canonical branch and cannot reflect non-main workflow runs.
findings.finding: The CI badge AC does not specify that the GitHub Actions badge URL must include a `branch=main` query parameter. Without it, GitHub's badge endpoint serves the status for the most recently triggered branch — which could be any non-feature branch push (test.yml runs on all branches except `feature/**`), producing a badge that intermittently reflects non-main CI state instead of the canonical branch.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — 'CI status' badge / What section: 'the test.yml workflow status badge from the GitHub mirror'
findings.suggested_fix: Update the AC to read: 'CI badge URL uses `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/workflows/test.yml/badge.svg?branch=main` and links to `https://github.com/navapbc/rebar/actions/workflows/test.yml?query=branch%3Amain`'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a brief positive rationale for the shields.io choice (e.g., 'shields.io provides a consistent visual style across all four badge types and is the de-facto OSS standard for PyPI metadata badges') so the implementer understands the constraint.
findings.finding: The plan does not state a positive rationale for choosing shields.io as the badge provider (vs. PyPI's own native badge endpoint or GitHub's built-in badge styling). The 'Why' section motivates the badges themselves but not the shields.io mechanism.
findings.location: What / files section — 'PyPI version (shields.io pypi/v/nava-rebar), Python versions (pypi/pyversions/nava-rebar), license (Apache-2.0)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
